question: Our delivery robots operate on chaotic schedules, sometimes they run continuous deep discharges, and other times they sit idle for hours. I have operational profiles with massive variations in rest periods and intrinsic cell variability. I need you to ingest this full operational profile (charge, discharge, and rest time vectors) and predict the End-of-Discharge (EOD) for the next 10 upcoming cycles across this varied group. Give me the MAE for these short-term EOD predictions. My dispatchers need a dashboard showing the exact minute each robot will hit its voltage threshold so we stop leaving bots stranded on the sidewalk.
